_README: DEMAND SYSTEM — Inject Custom Studies into the Worklist
WHAT IS THIS?
This file lets you tell the system: 'I want a specific exam to appear
in the worklist with specific characteristics and specific timing.'
The system checks this file every 30-60 seconds. Any new (unprocessed)
requests will be picked up and executed automatically.
HOW TO ADD A NEW DEMAND:
1. Add a new entry to the 'requests' array below.
2. Give it a unique 'id' (any string, e.g., 'demand-001').
3. Set 'processed' to false (the system sets it to true after handling).
4. Fill in the 'study' object with the exam characteristics you want.
5. Optionally set 'lifecycle_overrides' to control timing.
6. Optionally set 'cancel_at_stage' to cancel the exam at a specific stage.
7. Save the file. The system will pick it up within 30-60 seconds.
STUDY FIELDS (all are OPTIONAL — anything you don't specify will be randomly generated):
- patient_name: The patient's name (e.g., 'Smith, John A')
- modality: The imaging type. Must be one of: CT, CR, DX, MR, US, NM
- study_description: Short description (e.g., 'CT BRAIN STROKE W/O CONTRAST')
- priority: A number from 1 to 10 (10 = most urgent)
LIFECYCLE OVERRIDES (all are OPTIONAL — values are in SECONDS):
These let you control exactly how fast the exam moves through each stage.
If you don't set these, the system uses random delays from lifecycle.json.
- Introduced_to_Assigned: Seconds before it gets assigned to a radiologist
- Assigned_to_Reading: Seconds before the radiologist starts reading
- Reading_to_Pending_Approval: Seconds before it's submitted for approval
- Pending_Approval_to_Approved: Seconds before final approval
CANCEL AT STAGE (OPTIONAL):
Set this to cancel the exam at a specific stage instead of approving it.
Must be one of: 'Introduced', 'Assigned', 'Reading', 'Pending Approval'
Example: 'cancel_at_stage': 'Reading' means the exam will be cancelled
while it is being read, before it reaches approval.
IMPORTANT NOTES:
- You never need to delete old entries. Just add new ones at the bottom.
- The system marks processed entries as 'processed: true' automatically.
- If you want to re-run a demand, change its 'id' and set 'processed' to false.
- Times are in REAL wall-clock seconds. If you say 120, it really waits 2 minutes.

_examples._description: EXAMPLE 1: A simple exam that goes through the full lifecycle with default timing.
EXAMPLE 2: A STAT stroke exam. Assigned in 30 seconds, read in 1 minute, approved in 2 minutes total.
EXAMPLE 3: An MR exam that gets CANCELLED during the reading stage.
EXAMPLE 4: A specific patient exam with custom timing at every stage.
